author: Your Name
title: Why withhold calcium when phosphorus is severely elevated in tumor lysis syndrome?
answer: Giving calcium before lowering phosphorus risks acute calcium-phosphate precipitation, causing irreversible kidney damage and metastatic calcification.
detail: Lower phosphorus first using non-calcium phosphate binders like **sevelamer**, plus aggressive IV hydration.
The calcium-phosphorus product here is critically high; **metastatic calcification** occurs once it exceeds 55 to 60.
Treat **hypocalcemia** only after phosphorus drops below 7 to 8, unless seizures or severe arrhythmias occur.
Consider urgent **hemodialysis** if phosphorus stays above 10 despite binders or if acute kidney injury worsens.
